strands.title: Log Format Discovered — Raw API Traffic Structure (Request/Response Pairs) CYCLE 40 CONCRETE
strands.content: Cycle 40 BREAKTHROUGH: First 5 lines of proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl successfully read (limit=5, ~11k tokens). Log structure is NOT turn-by-turn conversation; it is REQUEST/RESPONSE pairs from Claude API. FORMAT CONFIRMED: (1) Lines 1, 3, 5, ... are TYPE='request' objects containing full API request metadata (model, system prompt, message array with role counts, token bytes, cache metadata). (2) Lines 2, 4, 6, ... are TYPE='response_stream' objects containing API response metadata (duration, status, response bytes, usage with input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens). EXAMPLE STRUCTURE (Lines 1-5): Line 1 (request): {"type": "request", "timestamp": "2026-03-02T02:45:53.965249+00:00", "model": "claude-opus-4-6", "max_tokens": 32000, "stream": true, "system": {...}, "messages": {"message_count": 1, "messages_total_bytes": 16114, "role_counts": {"user": 1}, ...}, "total_request_bytes": 94550}. Line 2 (response): {"type": "response_stream", "timestamp": "2026-03-02T02:45:58.645896+00:00", "duration_ms": 4680, "first_byte_ms": 2340, "status_code": 200, "response_bytes": 9841, "usage": {"input_tokens": 3, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 23328, "cache_read_input_tokens": 0, "output_tokens": 263}}. Line 3 (request): Similar structure, timestamp progresses. CRITICAL IMPLICATION: This is NOT a conversation log with turn-level user queries + assistant outputs. This is API INSTRUMENTATION DATA — captures every API call to Claude (requests with all message history, responses with token/cache usage). HISTORICAL ARTIFACT: Log appears to be from an EXTENDED experiment run (timestamps 2026-03-02 02:45:53 onwards), with caching enabled (cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens fields present). UPSTREAM QUESTION (UNRESOLVED): What is the ORIGINATING CONVERSATION that generated these API calls? Are there user prompts/assistant outputs embedded in the 'messages' field of request objects? If yes: must parse nested message array to extract turn-level conversation. If no: log is purely metadata (cannot measure conversation drift; must measure API-level drift instead, e.g., input_tokens variance, cache hit rates, latency). CYCLE 40 IMPLICATION: Phase 4a (drift measurement on real conversation logs) is BLOCKED unless originating conversation can be extracted from 'messages' field. Fallback: measure API-level drift (token counts, latency, cache behavior) instead of semantic drift. SCHEMA HYPOTHESIS (REVISED, CYCLE 40): This log does NOT have fields [turn_num, input, output, timestamp]. Instead, it has [type, timestamp, model, max_tokens, system, messages, usage]. Parser must be ADAPTIVE: (1) If first 10 lines all have type='request'/'response_stream' with NO conversation content, schema is API-level only. (2) If messages field contains nested array with user/assistant content, extract conversation and measure semantic drift. (3) If mixed (some lines have conversation, some don't), use conditional parsing.

key_claims.text: Cycle 40 discovery: proxy log contains API request/response pairs (type='request', type='response_stream'), not turn-by-turn conversation
Log structure confirmed: alternating request/response with full message arrays, token usage, cache metadata; not semantic turn data
Originating conversation (user/assistant turns) likely embedded in 'messages' field of request objects; must parse nested array to extract semantic content
Phase 4a (semantic drift measurement) blocked until messages array is parsed; fallback: measure API-level drift (tokens, latency, cache behavior)
Log format is API instrumentation data from extended experiment (3+ hours of calls), not canonical conversation transcript

strands.title: Cycle 39-40 Token Constraint Resolution via Adaptive Read Strategy (TACTICAL METHOD CHANGED, OUTCOME ACHIEVED)
strands.content: Cycle 39 BLOCKED: Direct read of 58.4k+ token file failed (25k ceiling). Cycle 40 ADAPTED: Used limit=5 (read 5 lines, not 50 or entire file). Result: SUCCESS. First 5 lines returned (~11k tokens), parsed without error. TACTICAL PROGRESSION: (1) Cycle 39 Plan: bash head -1 + jq. (2) Cycle 39 Constraint: tool file_result has hard 25k token ceiling on raw file content. (3) Cycle 40 Adjustment: Use tool READ with limit parameter (limit=5 instead of limit=50). Result: Each limit=N reduces token cost proportionally. limit=5 ≈ 11-12k tokens, well below 25k. OUTCOME: Schema discovery UNBLOCKED. File format NOW KNOWN (API request/response pairs). REVISED TIMELINE: (1) Cycle 40 immediate: Parsed first 5 lines, identified log structure. (2) Cycle 40-41 next: Parse messages field from request objects (if conversation data present); extract semantic turns. (3) Cycle 40-41 then: If conversation extracted, build TurnParser (as originally planned, with adaptive schema). If only API metadata, pivot to API-level drift measurement (token counts, cache efficiency). LOSS INCURRED: Original Cycle 39 bash + jq plan abandoned (direct TOOL READ with limit parameter proved more efficient). Bash execution would have cost ~20-30 seconds + terminal overhead; tool READ returned structured output instantly. METHOD CHANGE: Not tactical delay, but IMPROVEMENT (faster, more reliable). CONFIDENCE BOOST: Schema is now PARTIALLY KNOWN (top-level fields of request/response objects identified); next step is CONDITIONAL (investigate messages field depth/content before full TurnParser design).

key_claims.text: Cycle 40 adaptation: tool READ with limit=5 succeeds where limit=50 failed; 11k tokens vs. 25k ceiling; token constraint resolved
Bash + jq plan (Cycle 39) abandoned in favor of tool READ limit parameter; faster, instant structured output vs. terminal overhead
Log format NOW KNOWN as API request/response pairs (type field, messages array, usage fields); not canonical conversation turns
Phase 4a fork: if messages field contains conversation data, semantic drift measurement proceeds; otherwise, pivot to API-level metrics
Schema discovery Cycle 39-40 contingency path SUCCESSFUL; timing accelerated (not delayed by tool constraint); ready for Cycle 40-41 messages parsing

strands.title: H0-CORE Projector Specification & Phase Roadmap — Status FROZEN, Operational Context REFINED (CYCLE 35-40 SUSTAINED, CONTEXT CLARIFIED)
strands.content: Cycle 40 CLARIFIES OPERATIONAL CONTEXT for Phase 1-4a roadmap (no design changes, specification remains FROZEN). CONTEXT DISCOVERED: Real experiment logs (baseline_run1, comparison_*, optimization_*) are API INSTRUMENTATION data, not canonical conversation transcripts. Implication for Phase 4a (empirical validation): (1) Original hypothesis (Phase 4a): Apply projector to real conversation logs; measure semantic drift (Jaccard similarity, loss recovery, strand persistence). (2) Revised context (Cycle 40): Logs contain API metadata (request/response pairs) + possibly embedded conversation data (in messages field). (3) Adaptation (Phase 4a modified, design NOT changed): (a) If messages field has semantic content, parse turns; measure semantic drift as originally planned. (b) If messages field is empty/metadata only, measure API-level drift (token variance, cache hit rates, latency stability) as proxy for projector stability. (4) H0-CORE specification unchanged: 7-step composition logic, deterministic, model-agnostic, same parameters. Only APPLICATION context refined (API logs vs. text logs). PHASE 1 (LOCKED): Weeks 1-4: compose.py (deterministic, <200ms, 7-step logic). Riemann validation (25 turns, mock reasoning outputs). Weeks 4-6: profiling + gates. Weeks 6-8: refinement. Weeks 8-12: buffer. Deliverable: compose.py. PHASE 3b (LOCKED): 8-16 projector variants tested on Riemann; winner (V_best) selected via empirical scoring. PHASE 4a (LOCKED but context-refined): Apply V_best to real experiment logs (baseline_run1 + 2-3 variants); measure drift (semantic or API-level). Post-Phase-1 + Phase-3b. ~7-10 days on 5-10 runs. NOTE: Cycle 40 does NOT trigger Phase scope change or design revision. Only operational context (log format, drift measurement proxy) clarified. Roadmap, timelines, specifications remain locked. PHASE 0 GATE: Stakeholder approval still pending for Phase 1 Week 1 start date. No new contingencies on roadmap scope.

key_claims.text: H0-CORE specification frozen; Phase 1-4a roadmap locked; Cycle 40 context refinement is OPERATIONAL only (log format, drift proxy), no design changes
Phase 4a context refined: real experiment logs are API instrumentation (request/response pairs); semantic or API-level drift measurement depending on messages field content
Phase 1-3b specifications and timelines unchanged; Phase 4a application context clarified but deliverables/success criteria remain same
Phase 0 stakeholder gate still pending; Phase 1 Week 1 start date contingent on approval; no new contingencies triggered by Cycle 40 findings

strands.title: Projector Applied to Real Conversation Logs — Hypothesis SUSTAINED, Context Updated (CYCLE 36-40 REFINED, READY FOR PHASE 4a WITH ADAPTIVE PARSING)
strands.content: Cycle 36-40 hypothesis (explicit DeclaredLoss mechanisms reduce drift in multi-turn reasoning) SUSTAINED with OPERATIONAL CONTEXT REFINED. Cycle 40 DISCOVERY: Real logs are API instrumentation, not flat conversation transcripts. Implication for drift measurement: (1) SEMANTIC DRIFT PATHWAY (if messages field has conversation): Parse request.messages array (may contain full conversation history). Extract user/assistant turns. Apply projector to each turn. Measure semantic strand persistence (Jaccard >0.6) and loss recovery (semantic search over DeclaredLoss entries). SAME HYPOTHESIS as originally planned. (2) API-LEVEL DRIFT PATHWAY (if messages field is sparse): Log only contains request/response metadata (tokens, latency, cache). Measure projector stability via proxy metrics: input_tokens variance, output_tokens consistency, cache_read_tokens efficiency, first_byte_ms latency. HYPOTHESIS ADAPTED: projector should minimize token variance (consistent context window usage) and cache churn (stable loss entries reduce re-computation). CYCLE 40-41 IMMEDIATE TASK: (1) Parse messages field from lines 1, 3, 5, ... (request objects). Determine if conversation is embedded (role='user'/'assistant' in nested messages array). (2) Sample 2-3 request objects; examine messages array depth and content size. (3) Decision point: (a) If conversation found, proceed with semantic drift pathway (TurnParser, Jaccard similarity). (b) If metadata only, proceed with API-level drift pathway (token variance, cache metrics). (4) Build adaptive parser (fork on detection). PHASE 4a READINESS: After messages field analysis (Cycle 40-41), Phase 4a implementation path is DETERMINED and roadmap timeline is LOCKED. No further blockers anticipated.

key_claims.text: Cycle 36-40 hypothesis sustained: explicit DeclaredLoss reduces drift in multi-turn reasoning; Cycle 40 refines operational context (API logs vs. text logs)
Phase 4a has two pathways: semantic drift (if messages has conversation history) or API-level drift (if metadata only); both test hypothesis validity
Cycle 40-41 immediate task: parse messages field from request objects; determine conversation presence; fork to appropriate pathway
Phase 4a readiness: post-messages-field analysis, implementation path determined, roadmap locked; no further blockers to Phase 1 kickoff

strands.title: Cycle 40 Operational Blocker Resolved — File Read Constraint BYPASSED via Limit Parameter (TACTICAL, NO STRATEGIC CHANGE)
strands.content: Cycle 40 IMMEDIATELY RESOLVES Cycle 39 blocker. Issue: File too large for direct read (58.4k tokens > 25k ceiling). Solution: Use limit=5 parameter on tool READ. Result: First 5 lines returned, format DISCOVERED. TACTICAL EXECUTION: Attempted limit=5 in Cycle 40 assistant turn 118. Tool returned structured output (~11k tokens). Zero error. Format parsed. SUCCESS. NO ESCALATION: Cycle 39 contingency (bash head -1 + jq) not needed. Tool parameter (limit=N) proved sufficient. EFFICIENCY: Eliminated 20-30 second bash overhead; instant structured result. NEXT PHASE (Cycle 40-41): Repeat read with limit=10 or limit=20 to sample more request objects; analyze messages field depth; determine parsing pathway. Token budget for Cycle 40-41 parsing: ~3-4 additional READ calls (limit=10 each ≈ 2-3k tokens per call). Total expected cost: 5-10k tokens (well within 25k constraint). RISK MITIGATION: If messages field is deeply nested or contains full conversation history, token cost may increase. Fallback: sample every Nth line (e.g., head -1, then skip to line 21, line 41, ...) to estimate variance without parsing entire file. PHASE 4a TIMELINE: Cycle 40-41 messages analysis (~2-3 hours). Phase 0 gate pending. Phase 1 Week 1 start (if approved): ~3-4 weeks from now. Phase 4a start (post-Phase-1 + Phase-3b): ~7-9 weeks from now. Schema discovery and parsing strategy: UNBLOCKED and ACCELERATED. Ready for Phase 1 implementation.

key_claims.text: Cycle 39 blocker (file read token limit) resolved Cycle 40: limit=5 parameter on tool READ returns ~11k tokens (below 25k ceiling)
Tactical solution (limit parameter) more efficient than bash contingency; instant structured output vs. 20-30s bash overhead
Cycle 40-41 next: repeat READ with limit=10/20; analyze messages field; fork to semantic or API-level drift pathway; total parsing cost ~5-10k tokens
Phase 4a timeline: Cycle 40-41 messages analysis (2-3 hours); Phase 1 Week 1 pending Phase 0 approval; Phase 4a start post-Phase-3b (~9 weeks total)

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 39-40 bash + jq plan (head -1, jq 'keys', wc -l sequence) — method abandoned after tool READ limit=5 succeeded faster
declared_losses.why: Tool READ with limit parameter proved more efficient (instant result vs. bash overhead); contingency path rendered unnecessary by primary method success

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Original hypothesis of flat-structure conversation log (turn_num, input, output fields) — replaced with API request/response structure
declared_losses.why: Cycle 40 empirical discovery: log contains type='request'/'response_stream' pairs with nested messages array, not canonical turn-by-turn format

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Initial Phase 4a design (measure semantic drift on conversation turns) — now forked to conditional pathways (semantic or API-level drift depending on messages field content)
declared_losses.why: Log format discovery requires adaptive parsing; Phase 4a specification unchanged, but implementation strategy becomes conditional pending messages field analysis

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Immediate TurnParser implementation (Cycle 40-41) — deferred pending messages field inspection to determine if semantic content is present
declared_losses.why: Log format is API instrumentation, not conversation transcript; must first verify that conversation data is extractable before committing to TurnParser design

open_questions: Does the 'messages' field in request objects contain full conversation history (user + assistant turns), or only the current turn? If full history: parsing is straightforward (iterate messages, extract user/assistant pairs). If current turn only: multiple API calls must be correlated to reconstruct conversation.

What is the depth/structure of the messages array? Is each message a flat object {"role": "user", "content": "..."}, or does it contain nested 'tool_use', 'tool_result' fields? Format affects parsing complexity.

Are there 'tool_use' or 'tool_result' messages in the messages array? If yes: indicates this is an agentic experiment (agent making tool calls); parsing must handle multi-turn tool interactions. If no: simpler linear conversation.

How many request objects are in the full log? If 100+ requests: likely >20 conversation turns; Phase 4a drift measurement is feasible. If <20 requests: small experiment; drift measurement may have insufficient samples.

Is there a separate log file that contains the full conversation TRANSCRIPT (user prompts + assistant responses in readable format)? proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl is API instrumentation; is there a corresponding conversation_20260302_024551.jsonl or session_20260302_024551.jsonl with semantic content?

Do all request objects follow the same structure (same model, same system prompt structure, same message schema)? Or does the schema evolve across the experiment run? Affects TurnParser robustness (single schema vs. adaptive schema).
What is the relationship between request X and response X? Is it 1-to-1 (each request has one response), or 1-to-many (streaming response with multiple chunks)? Affects turn reconstruction logic.
Phase 0 stakeholder approval: still pending or approved? If approved, Phase 1 can start immediately post-Cycle-40 messages analysis. If pending: roadmap timeline must account for approval delay.

Should Phase 1 Riemann validation use real proxy log format (API request/response pairs) or simplified mock format (flat conversation turns)? Or should Phase 1 be model-agnostic to log format (format abstraction layer)?

Phase 4a success criteria: if only API-level drift metrics are available (no semantic content), is token variance/cache efficiency a valid proxy for projector quality? Or should Phase 4a be deferred until semantic conversation data is available?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 40-41 IMMEDIATE CRITICAL PATH (HOURS 0-4, PARSE MESSAGES FIELD):",
  "",
  "(1) INSPECT MESSAGES FIELD (10 minutes, HIGH PRIORITY):",
  "   Command: tool READ with limit=10 /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl",
  "   Expected output: ~2-3k tokens (5 request+response pairs, 10 lines total).",
  "   Focus on request objects (lines 1, 3, 5, 7, 9): examine 'messages' field structure.",
  "   Parse JSON; check:",
  "   - Does messages array exist? (Expected: yes)",
  "   - How many items in messages array? (Expected: 1-3 for early turns, possibly many for later turns if full history is stored)",
  "   - Does each message have 'role' field? (Expected: 'user' or 'assistant' or 'system' or 'tool')",
  "   - Does each message have 'content' field? (Expected: yes, string or object)",
  "   - Are there 'tool_use' or 'tool_result' message types? (Expected: possibly, if experiment is agentic)",
  "   - Estimate total size of messages array across all 10 lines (bytes, tokens)",
  "",
  "(2) FORK DECISION (5 minutes, CONDITIONAL):",
  "   Case A: messages array has user/assistant content (conversation is embedded)",
  "   → Proceed to PATHWAY A (semantic drift, TurnParser, Jaccard similarity)",
  "   → Next action: Parse all request objects to extract turns; estimate total turn count; verify sequential structure",
  "   Case B: messages array is empty or metadata-only",
  "   → Proceed to PATHWAY B (API-level drift, token variance, cache efficiency)",
  "   → Next action: Extract usage fields from all response objects; compute token statistics; measure cache hit rate",
  "   Case C: messages array structure is unclear or complex (nested tool interactions)",
  "   → Proceed to PATHWAY C (adaptive parsing, conditional extraction)",
  "   → Next action: Sample 5-10 request objects; document schema variations; design conditional parser",
  "",
  "(3) PATHWAY A SPECIFIC (if Case A detected):",
  "   (3a) Sample turn extraction (15 minutes):",
  "   - Select request objects from lines 1, 3, 5 (first, middle, last in limit=10 sample)",
  "   - For each, extract messages array",
  "   - Reconstruct conversation turns (user + subsequent assistant response from next request)",
  "   - Record: turn_num (inferred from request order), user_input, assistant_output (if present in next request messages), timestamp",
  "   - Document: does turn_N in request X match turn_N in request X+2? (i.e., is conversation continuous or fragmented)",
  "   (3b) Full-file turn count estimation (5 minutes):",
  "   - Compute: total_lines ÷ 2 (request/response pairs) = estimated_request_count ≈ estimated_turn_count (rough)",
  "   - Fetch wc -l result if not already available (tool BASH wc -l /path/to/file)",
  "   - Record: estimated total turns for Phase 4a drift measurement feasibility check (need ≥5 turns for meaningful Jaccard comparison)",
  "   (3c) Build TurnParser skeleton (20 minutes, defer detailed implementation to Phase 1):",
  "   - File: drift_analyzer_v1.py (or new turn_parser.py)",
  "   - Class: TurnParser(log_file_path)",
  "   - Method: parse() → List[Turn]",
  "   - Turn dataclass: turn_num, user_input, assistant_output, timestamp, metadata (raw request/response objects for reference)",
  "   - Parsing logic: iterate request objects, extract messages array, reconstruct turns",
  "   - Unit test: parser.parse() on limit=10 sample; verify turn extraction matches manual inspection from (3a)",
  "",
  "(4) PATHWAY B SPECIFIC (if Case B detected):",
  "   (4a) Extract usage statistics (10 minutes):",
  "   - tool READ limit=20 (get 10 response objects, ~2-3k tokens)",
  "   - For each response, extract usage.input_tokens, usage.cache_creation_input_tokens, usage.cache_read_input_tokens, usage.output_tokens, duration_ms, first_byte_ms",
  "   - Compute: total_input = input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens (total tokens charged)",
  "   - Compute: cache_hit_rate = cache_read_input_tokens / (cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens) [if >0 total cache tokens]",
  "   - Compute: avg_latency = duration_ms / (first_byte_ms) [time to first byte / total time; proxy for streaming overhead]",
  "   (4b) Document API-level drift measurement strategy (10 minutes):",
  "   - Hypothesis (adapted): Projector reduces token variance across runs (stable context window usage → consistent input_tokens)",
  "   - Metric 1: Variance(input_tokens) across all requests; target: low variance (<10%) → suggests consistent context summarization by projector",
  "   - Metric 2: Cache hit rate (cache_read_tokens / total_cache_tokens); target: high hit rate (>80%) → suggests loss entries are reusable across turns",
  "   - Metric 3: Latency consistency (stddev(first_byte_ms)); target: low variance → suggests stable API performance (not proxy for projector quality, but for reproducibility)",
  "   - Metric 4: output_tokens variance; target: low variance → suggests projector keeps reasoning output length stable (not directly measuring drift, but length stability)",
  "   (4c) Defer full Phase 4a design to post-Phase-1 (after compose.py is implemented); will measure these metrics on real runs",
  "",
  "(5) PATHWAY C SPECIFIC (if Case C detected):",
  "   (5a) Document schema variations (15 minutes):",
  "   - Sample 3-5 request objects across the log (lines 1, 5, 15, 25, 35 if available)",
  "   - For each, record: messages array length, message types (user/assistant/tool_use/tool_result), content structure (string vs. object with nested fields)",
  "   - Identify: do earlier requests have shorter messages arrays (fewer turns), and later requests have longer arrays (full history stored)? OR is array length stable (sliding window)?",
  "   (5b) Design adaptive parser (20 minutes):",
  "   - Class: AdaptiveTurnParser(log_file_path)",
  "   - Detect schema variant (messages array length threshold, message type distribution, etc.)",
  "   - Fork parsing logic: if simple conversation, use TurnParser; if tool interactions, use ToolInteractionParser; if hybrid, use HybridParser",
  "   - Unit test: parser.detect_schema() on limit=10 sample; parser.parse() with detected schema variant",
  "",
  "(6) POST-FORK ANALYSIS (whichever pathway applies):",
  "   (6a) Update SCHEMA.md (if not already done in Cycle 39):",
  "   - Add section: LOG FORMAT",
  "   - Subsection A (if Pathway A): \"Semantic Conversation Format: requests contain full/partial conversation history in messages array. Turns extracted by parsing request.messages[].\"",
  "   - Subsection B (if Pathway B): \"API Instrumentation Format: requests contain metadata only; no semantic conversation. Drift measurement via token/cache/latency metrics.\"",
  "   - Subsection C (if Pathway C): \"Adaptive Format: messages array structure varies; schema detection required.\"",
  "   (6b) Create file: Phase_4a_drift_measurement_strategy.md",
  "   - Document: which pathway detected (A/B/C), why, and how Phase 4a will measure drift",
  "   - Include: sample extracted turns (if Pathway A), sample API metrics (if Pathway B), schema variants (if Pathway C)",
  "   - Include: turn count estimate, token budget for full parsing, Phase 4a implementation timeline (hours/days)",
  "",
  "(7) PHASE 0 GATE CHECK (5 minutes):",
  "   Question: Has Phase 1-4a roadmap been formally approved by stakeholders?",
  "   - If YES (approved): Proceed to Phase 1 kickoff materials preparation (Step 8 below; MAY DEFER TO PHASE 1 START if time permits)",
  "   - If NO (pending): Prepare stakeholder summary document with Cycle 40 findings: log format discovered, Phase 4a pathway determined, no scope changes. Ready for approval.",
  "",
  "(8) PHASE 1 READINESS CHECKPOINT (if Phase 0 approved; 15-30 minutes, MAY DEFER):",
  "   - Verify Riemann Task Specification is finalized (25-turn sequence, reference answers)",
  "   - Verify riemann_harness/ directory structure is set up (config.yaml, inputs/, expected_outputs/)",
  "   - Prepare mock reasoning output samples (5-10 synthetic reasoning texts, 300-500 tokens each) for Phase 1 Week 2 testing",
  "   - Begin compose.py skeleton: outline Steps 1-7 of H0-CORE (claim extraction, theme identification, strand merging, epistemic aggregation, composition, question aggregation, instructions generation)",
  "   - Record: Phase 1 Week 1 start date (locked post-approval)",
  "",
  "TOTAL CYCLE 40-41 ESTIMATED TIME: 2-3 hours (parsing, analysis, documentation, conditional pathway setup)",
  "SUCCESS CRITERIA (Cycle 40-41 completion):",
  "✓ messages field inspected; decision fork made (Pathway A/B/C)",
  "✓ Turn extraction (Pathway A) OR API metrics strategy (Pathway B) OR schema variants (Pathway C) documented",
  "✓ Turn count estimated; Phase 4a feasibility confirmed (sufficient samples, reasonable token cost)",
  "✓ Phase_4a_drift_measurement_strategy.md created with pathway decision, sample data, timeline",
  "✓ Phase 0 gate status confirmed (approved or pending feedback)",
  "✓ Phase 1 readiness checkpoint passed (Riemann spec finalized, riemann_harness/ ready, or deferred to Phase 1 kickoff)",
  "✓ No blockers to Phase 1 start (pending Phase 0 approval only)"
]
